calendar.matters:
Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Nov-10-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Richard A. Canatella, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Bebe E. Paulino, who was placed under oath and examined.
Edward Paulino, a witness for plaintiff, was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant Robert Paulino.
Default was entered against defendant Robert Paulino on October 8, 2008.
Dismissal with prejudice as to defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. was filed on January 30, 2009.
Dismissal without prejudice of cross-complaint of Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. was filed on February 17, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed today, November 10, 2009.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Bebe E. Paulino, and against the Defaulted Defendant Robert Paulino as follows:

Damages/Principal - $665,000.00.
Attorney's Fees - $15,000.00.

Judgment in the amount of $680,000.00. was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Notice Of Intention To Move For New Trial And Motion For New Trial
Off Calendar Nov-10-2009 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Aug-25-2009 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Richard A. Canatella, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Beebe E. Paulino, who was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant Robert Paulino.
Dismissal with prejudice as to defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. in Trust for the Benefit of the Certificate Holders of Asset Backed Securities Corporation Home Equity Loan Trust Series OOMC 2005-HE6 was filed on January 30, 2009.
Cross-complaint of Wells Fargo Bank, N.A., et al. was dismissed without prejudice on February 17, 2009.
Default was filed against defendant Robert Paulino on October 30, 2008.
Doe defendants have not been dismissed as of today's date.

The Court cannot proceed with today's hearing as further documentation is required by plaintiff. Accordingly, this matter is taken OFF CALENDAR.
